desc:
One of the largest bridges in Moria crossed Durin's Chimney here, but it fell
into ruin long ago and now only a small portion of it can be seen on the eastern
edge of the chimney. In its place, the orcs have a erected a ramshackle span
that appears none too sturdy. To the east, the main thoroughfare has been
blocked with a formidable barricade - probably demarcating a boundary between
two tribes. To the west, the bridge continues, and beyond the passageway appears
clear.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
This spindly bridge - obviously not of Dwarven make - spans the wide shaft of
Durin's Chimney. To the west, a wide road pushes onward toward the western
portion of Khazad-dum, while to the east the narrow bridge comes to an end in
front of an imposing barricade.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
Here, Durin's Way intersects another large Avenue. The markings indicate that
this north-south passage is dedicated to Yavanna, the wife of Aule the Maker. To
the east, the rock walls suddenly disappear and Durin's Way meets the Chimney of
Durin -- a great open area in the heart of Caradhras. Apparently a great bridge
once spanned this abyss, but it has long been destroyed, and in its place a
shoddy bridge of orkish manufacturer connects both sides of the Chimney.
name: Before Durin's Chimney

desc:
This section of Durin's Way is cloaked in utter darkness. Although the stretch
of road ahead of you appears silent and barren, you get a strong feeling that
something is watching your every move. The Way travels both east and west
through the gloom here.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Durin's Way continues to progress both east and west through the heart of the
mountain. Battered walls and defiled statuary lend silent witness to the most
recent outrages committed by the current occupants against once-proud Moria.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The once glorious road known as Durin's Way intersects a large, ruined avenue
here. Even in the darkness, you can see signs of the grandeur and majesty that
Moria once possessed, lessened now by time and ill-fortune. Durin's Way runs
east-west through the mountain, while the avenue stretches north into some
boulders and south from here.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Both to the east and west, Durin's Way stretches grandly into the darkness. The
cold and hard stone floor has been worn down by countless feet, first Naugrim
and now Orkish. The walls stare at you, unchanging and featureless.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
You walk along the once mighty and glorious road known as Durin's Way. Only a
hint of its past grandeur now remains, but the sheer scale of this work is
awe-inspiring. The Way continues east and west, but your attention is drawn to a
wide stone door just north of here.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
You have entered a cavernous complex of rooms. Stretching away on all sides,
small rooms are carved into the stone walls. A myriad of walkways, ledges and
stairs provide access to these rooms. Roads converge on this place from all
directions indicating a major junction. The high domed ceiling rises far above
you and sometimes disappears from sight, either rushing up into impenetrable
blackness, or vanishing behind huge stone columns. Twisting and turning, the
passages and rooms are truly impressive and you marvel at the magnificence of it
all.
name: The Cavern Crossroads

desc:
The huge underground passageway slowly bores deeper and deeper into the depths
of the mountain and you marvel at the size of this immense tunnel. What once was
the dwarves' major highway is now silent except for the echoing sound your boots
make as they strike the stony ground. The road disappears into darkness to the
west and to the east you can make out a distinct opening in the otherwise
endless tunnel.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Continuing its east-west passage, the wide avenue ploughs on under the great
mountain. At times you have to strain your eyes to make out the ceiling high
above you, and at some places it even disappears completely in the darkness
above. As you peer westwards it seems the hard rock walls open up even more,
maybe to make way for another passageway.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Here the two giant roadways Durin's Way and Roin's Scar meet up with another.
While the size of Durin's Way alone is impressive, the joining of these two
great underground avenues makes a truly magnificent sight. Huge, echoing chasms
and towering walls of rock extend north and south while a wide avenue swings out
across the chasms on a series of massive stone bridges. The vast tunnel that was
once the major highway of the dwarves opens up to the east and west.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
A large chamber straddles the grand avenue. Large, defaced columns line the
walls and most seem to have been carved with Dwarven warriors caparisoned for
war. These illustrations must once have been a beautiful sight, but now the
passage of time and the desecration done by the caverns' current inhabitants
have made them look worn and abraded. The avenue of Durin exits east and north,
and to the west a narrow, seemingly bottomless, chute descends into the deeps.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The road tunnels on through the depths, bending from west to south. The hard
ground is strewn with small rocks and boulders on either side of the roadway, as
if a minor cave-in has been cleared up recently. Looking closer you realise the
rocks must come from a fallen stone column now laying pushed to one side of the
cavernous tunnel.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The grand roadway slowly bends from north to east, plunging onward through the
dark deeps. The air is slightly chilly and damp, sending a small shiver down
your spine. You hear the sound of dripping water, and looking closer at the rock
walls you see tiny streams of water trickling down from the ceiling to disappear
through near invisible cracks in the floor. Though the mining of these caves has
long since ceased, you can see distinct signs of the work that once filled these
regions with the din of mine-working.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The huge avenue lumbers onward under the mountain, running north and south.
Looking in either direction all you see is utter darkness and you wonder if this
road is ever going to end. Broken and long-since useless mining tools lie tossed
into a cleft in the east wall of the tunnel and a pile of boulders and large
rocks rest against the western part of the cavern wall. Clearly, whoever now
inhabits these underground passageways cares nothing for keeping this grand
creation of the dwarves in good condition, leaving things in disarray, with
never a thought to set things straight.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Winding away under the mighty Silvertine, the avenue ponderously bends west to
south in this huge chamber. The silent, brooding atmosphere is broken only by
the sounds you make yourself and the occasional muffled clattering sound coming
from the distant west. You pause to try and discern the source of the sound, but
hear nothing except deep silence.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
A great chamber, hewn by the forces of nature and by the industrious hands of
the dwarves is served by three main roadways. Running east is the widest avenue,
north a narrower road disappears into the gloom, and west, Durin's Way runs its
course onward toward some distant goal.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Here the wide avenue twists from south to east, slowly winding onward into the
heart of the mountain. Your footsteps echo through the cavern, but that is all
you can hear in this dark and foreboding place. A faint breeze carries a damp
smell through the air, suggesting that it must have been a very long times since
fires warmed these underground caverns.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
This majestic, huge tunnel, the major thoroughfare of Moria, turns from west to
north here. What once must have been a well kept and magnificent roadway now
lies disused. The dwarves that once lived here are no longer around to keep
things in good condition, and who or whatever now inhabits these dungeons
clearly seem to enjoy destroying its surroundings much more than expanding upon
them, or even maintaining them.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
This area of the huge tunnel is filled with several small tunnels darting off in
different directions, narrow clefts bored into the rock and the like. The major
roadway of Durin's Way runs through the cavern east-west. Even before entering
any of the small passageways that depart from the main roadway here, you notice
that fallen rubble has filled all of them, making them all impossible to travel.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Looking around this large natural cavern you notice a small dried out pool lying
directly below an opening in the western wall, where the water must have once
tumbled down. As you look closer, you see that this small spring was probably
once fed by a small underground stream emerging from the rock just above the
pool, forming a small but beautiful waterfall. Now only scattered drops of water
form at the mouth of the hole and fall down into the shallow spring. The remains
of a torn waterskin lie beside the now empty pool, as well as a smashed wooden
cup. Winding onward under the peaks, Durin's Way turns gently in its path, from
east to south.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Slowly turning from west to north, Durin's Way leads on through the darkness.
The roadway is somewhat narrower here, but the ceiling still looms high
overhead. As you look at the blackened rock walls, you think that you catch a
faint glint of gold. Upon closer inspection, you realise that it was nothing
more than your mind playing a trick on you. No veins of gold are visible in this
part of the tunnel, and you realise that this part of the caverns was never
mined, and was only used as a thoroughfare.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The roadway passes through a large cavern, split by a narrow, black chasm. A
couple of stone steps lead up to a wide stone arch, on which the great road
spans the huge gap of the yawning abyss below. The road exits the chamber east
and south. Two towering stone pillars frame the archway on either side of the
chasm. All of these columns show signs of recent attempted destruction. The
vandals have hacked away at the pillars with great force, leaving them ragged
and hacked, but still intact.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The grand, wide avenue of Durin bores on through the mountain's fastness,
running north-south. Once worn by the passage of thousands of dwarves, the
ground at the centre of the tunnel shows only faint signs of abrasion. These
tunnels have now long since been abandoned by those who built them. Still, you
are reminded that they are far from empty by the sound of drums beating in
rhythm far in the distance. Continually building and fading like a giant heart,
the ominous sound appears to come steadily closer. As the deep beating suddenly
disappears completely, the tunnel is left in an eerie silence.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The grand roadway underneath the mountain, Durin's way, drives onward in this
low-ceilinged part of the tunnel. It bends in its course, veering from west to
north. The ceiling dips low in the middle of the passageway, and framing this
natural arch are two statues. Depicting a pair of great Dwarven warriors, these
two images of stone have been the victims of brute vandalism. Both have had
their hands and arms cut off where these protruded from the main body. The heads
of both statues have been violently smashed from atop the torso, and to one side
of the corridor, you find one of the heads, badly battered.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The narrow passage comes to a halt as the only apparent exit, besides east,
rises up into darkness. The round chute that soars upwards from this small
chamber looks impossible to ascend, even to the most adept climber. As you stand
staring up into the deep blackness above, a small drop of water comes falling
out of it, landing on your forehead. You turn to search for other plausible
exits, but find none.
name: At the Bottom of a Chute

desc:
This cramped passage runs due east-west, widening slightly to the east and
becoming even more narrow going west. It has a slight decline downwards to the
west and as you look in that direction, the grim darkness makes it look as if
the tunnel suddenly dropped out of sight. Carefully coming closer, you see that
the passage actually continues there, the solid rock at your feet not plunging
into any grim abyss, as you had first thought.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
This very narrow passage runs due east-west and has the look of a little used
mining tunnel. To the east it would appear to emerge into a cavern of sorts.
Signs of the work of the Dwarven miners can still be faintly seen, as they once
burrowed their way into the mountain.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
Standing in the southwestern quarter of a large chamber, this place is dominated
by the wide road entering from the south. A narrower passage runs away west and
to the east and north, the cavern stretches away into darkness. In the corner of
this section of the huge cave rests a couple of mid-sized boulders that, upon
closer inspection, make an excellent place to sit down and rest your weary
bones.
name: A Large Junction Chamber

desc:
You stand at the southeastern corner of a huge chamber. The ceiling rises up
into dizzying heights and is soon lost from sight. Judging by the angle of the
natural rock walls, and the eerie echoing in this chamber, you wonder if there
is a ceiling at all. To the east a wide roadway enters and to the south a crude,
narrowish passage runs away. As you look around to examine your surroundings,
you cannot help but notice a faint but nevertheless disgusting smell coming from
the south tunnel.
name: A Large Junction Chamber

desc:
The road upon which you stand runs climbing from west to east. Well crafted, the
road has groove marks in it indicating some importance in past times. While the
corridor climbing into darkness in the east seems to look exactly the same as
the one in which you are standing now, the exit to the west appears to open into
a large cave of some sort; much larger than the simple tunnel you are now
standing in.
name: Gently Sloping Roadway

desc:
Bending from west to south, the roadway travels onwards and upwards - or
downwards to the west, depending on which way you should decide to go. A faint
whiff of some sickly smell drifts past you in the gentle breeze that floats
through here. Shifting your gaze from the ground in front of you to the walls at
your sides, you notice something you either have not encountered earlier on this
road, or simply have not paid attention to: Placed at regular intervals in this
cavernous tunnel are the remains of several iron sconces. These appear heavily
rusted and most of them are unusable, smashed and broken by trolls or orcs.
name: Gently Sloping Roadway

desc:
The road runs onwards, north-south, and continues at a steady incline as it
traverses the dark depths. You hear a muted shuffling sound coming from the
north, where the roadway slowly descends into blackness. You peer intently ahead
of you, trying to make out anything distinct, but the darkness seem endless and
you see nothing.
name: Gently Sloping Roadway

desc:
The road upon which you stand turns sharply here, from north to east. To the
east it climbs, and northwards it descends into the Deeps. The rough rock walls
of the tunnel rise at a sharp angle, making this part of the corridor almost
V-shaped.
name: Gently Sloping Roadway

desc:
To the east of this tunnel the passageway opens up to what you think must be a
much wider set of caverns. At the eastern end of this cave, you notice that the
echoing your feet make as they strike the ground change; it clearly indicates a
sizeable enlargement of the tunnel, and since the continual darkness of this
place has made you rely more on your sense of hearing, you are inclined to trust
your conclusions. The road you are walking on runs due east-west and has a
steady incline up to the east.
name: Gently Sloping Roadway

desc:
You are at the southern end of a great cavern, its northern extremes lost in the
darkness. A wide road stands out amongst the myriad of narrow stairs and
bridges, running away to the north. To the south the road immediately turns west
and enters a tunnel. A slight breeze is in the air and in the distance you hear
something sounding like a coarse scream echoing through the caverns.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
You stand in the midst of a vast underground canyon. Below, the ground plunges
away into unseen depths. A wide roadway traverses the caverns north-south,
running along on a series of arches, hopping from pillar to pillar. Giant
stalagmites, many of which support the arched walkways, stand everywhere in the
caverns - those at the end of your field of vision looking like ghosts of
hulking giants. Staring intently at one of these huge columns, you seem to make
out movement at a distance. When you blink your eyes, it disappears, not to be
seen again.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
At the northern end of a colossal cavern, part of the great fault know as Roin's
Scar, the road upon which you travel turns from west to south. The air is cold
and damp and almost completely silent. The faint, soft sound of dripping water
forms a continuous background to other, periodical sounds like the occasional
falling rock or the strange rumbling or clattering from some unknown source.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
You are in a great cavern, travelling upon a wide roadway. The broad shelf upon
which you stand plunges into unseen depths just to the northeast. The road
arching out east over the depths also runs north, hugging the western wall.
Carved out of the solid rock, these walkways traverse large parts of the great
fault-line known as Roin's Scar.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
Far above, the ceiling is lost in the dark; below, the distant sounds of water
gurgling and splashing in the great depths of the caverns echo. The road on
which you stand runs from north to south along the western edge of the chasm.
The walkways are made of smooth rock and you cannot help but marvel at the
ingenuity of the dwarves that once built this roadway. Whatever few handrails
there once might have been have long since been destroyed. You find evidence of
such railings, but seemingly, someone have torn them all apart.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
Vast echoing chasms drop away on either side as you stand on a wide platform.
The road arches away south on a great span. To the north it continues to run
along the ridge. Giant towering stalagmites reach up from the depths below,
while equally large stalactites loom from the hard rock ceiling above you.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
You are on a wide ledge at the south-westernmost point of this part of the
system of caverns and passages. Two huge stalagmites loom high above you, like a
pair of giant statues, welded to the southern rockface. The roadway continues
its progress north and east. Below you, and to the west, the gaping black depths
of the mountain seem like a bottomless abyss.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
Vast, eerily echoing darkness dominates all else. The floor simply disappears to
the north, making the mountain's heartrock at your back, south and west, seem
even more reassuringly solid. The road that emerges from the western rockface
runs away east, hugging the southern wall of the chasm. A narrow stair clings to
the precipice just north, winding its way down, presumably towards the echoing
thunder of some underground torrent. A roaring sound is rising from the depths
below, as well as a fine watery mist.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
The glum road bends from south to east as it travels under the mighty peaks of
the mountain above. You get a definite feeling that the air is colder here than
in other parts of the underground complex you have been passing through. An
eerie echoing rumble is coming from the east.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You stand on a wide, dark road whose smooth rock surface is pitted only in a few
scattered places. The tunnel twists and bends slightly during its north-south
course and the otherwise silent air is mingled with a faint rumbling sound,
coming from the north.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You are standing in a small damp cavern, feeling a gentle breeze drift from the
chute above and out through the only other exit west of here. Critically
appraising the smooth rock walls climbing up into darkness at a steep angle you
realise that ascending would be impossible. The slight trickling of water,
running down the walls of the circular shaft, make nothing to improve that
situation.
name: At the Bottom of a Chute

desc:
This small tunnel makes a sharp bend from the south to the east. You are forced
to watch your step to make sure you do not stumble and fall on any of the sharp
rocks that are strewn about this passage. The air is stirred by a steady, though
barely noticeable breeze coming from the east.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
You are at the northeastern corner of a large chamber. A narrow passage runs off
to the north, while wide flagstone extend south and west across the chamber
floor. The cavern itself looks to be natural but the ground has clearly seen
some extensive work, judging by the large flagstone tiles. The rough, rocky
walls look otherwise undisturbed by mortal hand. Standing in this huge cavern,
you suddenly hear a faint, high pitched scream echo through the tunnels from
somewhere in the distance.
name: A Large Junction Chamber

desc:
This fairly narrow tunnel has scarred walls and bits of trash all about. The
faint but readily discernible odour of something rotten pervades the air. The
nooks and crannies in the rough walls of this tunnel makes you shift your gaze
constantly. It would seem they make excellent places of hiding, and you dare not
leave any side unguarded, should something creep up on you.
name: Smelly Passage

desc:
The passage bends west to north, debris still scattered all about the floor. The
foul stench is getting stronger with every step towards the west and while it is
not yet insufferable, you wonder how any kind of creature could live in this
awful environment. You draw a quick breath of relief as a sudden gust of wind
blows through the tunnel, temporarily dispersing the foul fumes.
name: Smelly Passage

desc:
This smallish tunnel continues its course from east to west. Faeces are
noticeable among the various bits of cloth and bone that lie scattered about and
you start to realise where the putrid smell is coming from. Even though the
smell is what occupies most of your thoughts, your mind staggers at the sight of
what lies in a corner of the passageway: the rotting corpse of what once must
have been a small humanoid creature, maybe a hobbit, lies carelessly tossed at
the southern wall. Its head has been removed and in its place a much too large
Dwarven head made of stone has been set, obviously taken from a statue
somewhere. The image is a cruel mockery to all that is good and you fight for
control as a wave of nausea sweeps violently over you at the gruesome sight.
name: Smelly Passage

desc:
You are at the southern end of a cavern. Trash of all types and manner is strewn
about and the stench is really strong. This is obviously part of a lair whose
inhabitants do not care about tidiness... A narrowish passage runs east-west
through this part of the cavern and to the north, the chamber opens up into the
darkness.
name: The Southern End of a Large Cavern

desc:
The litter seems somewhat less in evidence here as the passage narrows and runs
away west. This tunnel has obviously seen less use than the one east of here,
and the moisture of the walls and the ceiling becomes more apparent as they are
now only a few feet from your face.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
The narrow passage suddenly comes to an abrupt end as solid rock walls block all
directions except back east, from where you came. It is probable that this might
be an old abandoned mining tunnel.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
A stinking pile of garbage is stacked up against the northern wall and a fading
cooking fire is in the centre of this chamber. Foul odours waft about in this
large domed cavern and a thin greasy film of brownish liquid covers the floor.
Each step makes a small sucking sound and you are disgusted as you imagine what
the stuff comes from. Large ugly looking flies of some sort buzz around you,
doing nothing to ease the discomfort of your battered senses. The cavern
continues to the south, while two tunnels exit to the east and west.
name: A Large Dirty Cavern

desc:
Carcasses of various natures lie about the feet of the walls of this natural
cave. Crude instruments of torture dot the floor and the large boulders that are
placed throughout the place. Bits of some unfortunate creature still seem to be
attached to these tools in some cases and rough pliers seem to dominate the
horrid collection. Propped against a rock in the far corner of the cavern you
see the badly beaten corpse of a hobbit. Horrible gaping wounds cover its body
and you catch a glimpse of severed fingers before you avert your eyes.
name: Cavern of Fun

desc:
This cave would seem to serve as the sleeping pit for the troll residents. Warg
furs and other skins lie strewn across the floor. The smell is terrible and you
cover your nose and mouth as you wander about this room. You can make out
several crude cots and also find a worn-out pot thrown into a corner. Rotten and
roughly jagged wooden planks stand against the natural rock walls and lie
scattered on the ground, their use a mystery. A few boulders also fill the
cavern and you quickly discard the idea of resting against one them, the awful
smell and general mess of this place effectively dissuading you.
name: Sleeping Area

desc:
The condition of the wide avenue has slightly improved at this point. Only a few
small blocks have fallen from the high roof to splinter on the floor below, the
walls here show no large cracks and the rest of the roof appears to be quite
stable. Still, one can never be too cautious when dealing with tons of stone
overhead, so you had better keep quiet and move along quickly. The avenue meets
Durin's Way just to the north and also steadily continues into the south.
name: Ruined Avenue

desc:
This section of the ancient avenue is in no better condition than those you have
seen before. Huge cracks cover the walls and some overhead stoneblocks seem
ready to drop at any moment. This is definitely not the place for shouting or
loud talk, unless you enjoy the feeling of being crushed to death. The ruined
avenue runs north and south from here.
name: Ruined Avenue

desc:
An interesting pattern formed by the cracks covering one of the walls here
attracts attention. The pattern appears to spell out the word D-O-O-M. Perhaps
this is mere coincidence or evidence of an overly active imagination, but it
does make one wonder. The avenue here was once wide and grand, and it does
appear to have met with great destruction a long time ago. You can follow the
ruined avenue north or proceed south through a portal into a large hall.
name: Ruined Avenue

desc:
This once magnificent great hall now lies in ruins. Large chunks of grey stone
have plummeted from the roof to block most of the hall, and shards of rusted
metal lie everywhere underfoot. The air here is both damp and dust-filled, a
most unpleasant combination which is difficult to breathe for long periods of
time. What used to be a wide avenue bends through the middle of this hall then
continues through large sculptured portals centred in the north and east walls.
name: Iron Hall

desc:
The damp air here lends a shiny lustre to the dark walls that border this wide
avenue. You wonder how long this place has existed, and exactly what happened
here that allowed orcs to destroy what must have been one of the most
magnificent places on Arda. There are bone fragments scattered everywhere here
that crack under your feet with every step you take. The avenue leads west
through an elaborately carved portal and continues east as far as the eye can
see.
name: Avenue of Iron

desc:
Blasted ruins of iron sprawl out from both sides of this wide avenue. Once-
proud Dwarven homes lie crushed beneath immense iron pillars which have been
toppled like tenpins. Twisted and melted iron statuary leers out from behind an
overturned fountain which blocks the centre of the avenue ahead. Many of the
standing structures are covered with strange marks which make no sense at all to
you. By skirting the ruins, it is possible to travel east or west from here.
name: Avenue of Iron

desc:
A fierce battle must have raged here within the past day, judging from the blood
and flesh that is strewn about this part of the Avenue. There are no humanoid
limbs or bodies here, but you do spot a pile of gigantic old bones and some huge
sharp teeth piled near a stone pillar just north of the road. Some large, dark
pools of water north of the pillar ripple ominously as you approach, and you
quickly decide to return to the relative safety of Iron Avenue. The Avenue leads
east and west from here, as it ever has.
name: Avenue of Iron

desc:
The massive strength displayed in the design of this great hall forms the
overwhelming impression upon you. The walls here are jet black, polished and
smooth as glass, and the vast roof far above is upheld by many mighty pillars of
stone. There are three dark black arches which lead out of this place onto wide
avenues travelling east, west and north.
name: Iron Hall

desc:
This narrow portion of the Avenue is choked by the many toppled black walls and
crumbling stone dwellings that once bordered the road. A large, rust-covered
iron dome which used to crown a small building nearby rests upside-down directly
in front of you. Everything here is covered by a fine layer of dust, and the air
is stale-smelling and very still. There has been no activity near this place in
many months at the very least. To the west lies a huge, rusted iron archway
which opens into a dark hall, while to the east there is much more debris
cluttering the Avenue.
name: Avenue of Iron

desc:
The Avenue of Iron has fallen into ruin and utter disrepair here, meaning that
your progress through here will be difficult at best. Many huge pits and chasms
open up in the roadway in front of you, and much of the surrounding overhead
stonework has worked its way loose then crashed down in great chunks. Because of
the obvious dangers present here, minions avoid travelling this way if possible,
and you should also exercise caution when you decide to continue onward to the
east or west.
name: Avenue of Iron

desc:
The environs here are shrouded in complete darkness. The road under your feet
still feels firm, but you tread cautiously in hope of avoiding any unseen crack
or pitfall. The air here is damp and warm, and carries the slight smell of
decaying flesh. Though you cannot see exactly where you are headed, you can make
your way along the Avenue generally north or south from this point.
name: Yavanna's Avenue

desc:
This section of Yavanna's Avenue is wide and clear of all debris. There are many
tracks here in the dust indicating that this way receives a lot of use, but
there does not seem to be too much activity here now. The road leads north
towards a huge chasm and south into darkness.
name: Yavanna's Avenue

desc:
The Avenue is very wide here and shows several subtle signs of graceful wear
from many years of hard use. Several now-abandoned structures near here appear
to be storehouses for goods, mining tools and stone destined for trade or sale.
Apparently, a lot of activity has taken place near here over the centuries, and
perhaps you are approaching a well-used nexus in the city. The road continues
south from here towards a huge chasm or runs north into some dim light.
name: Yavanna's Avenue

desc:
The Avenue receives some scant light from somewhere above here which illuminates
some fine stonework characteristic of the Moria dwarves. Many large faces or
masks holding a range of expressions have been sculpted in relief into the walls
bordering the road. This strikes you as curious, but serves to lighten your
mood. The way continues to the north and south into deeper shadow.
name: Yavanna's Avenue

desc:
This wide avenue leads directly north to the foot of a gigantic brass portal
which opens into a huge hall. The thoroughfare is still in excellent condition
to the south, and the seamless stonework here provides a fine example of the
supreme mastery the dwarves held in the art of roadbuilding.
name: Yavanna's Avenue

desc:
The scale and splendour of this titanic bronze greathall inspires wonder and
beggars description. Two rows of immense sculpted bronze columns support a great
copper-clad vaulted roof, now green with great age. Each thick column holds the
shattered remains of a great crystal lamp, whose radiance once bathed
gemstone-studded murals and walls with soft, warm light. Much beauty here has
been despoiled or ruined by age and decay, but enough remains to validate the
reputation of Moria dwarves as the most skilled masters of stonecraft and
metalwork ever to walk on Arda. The Bronze Hall stretches into the west, and you
can pass through wide bronze portals east and south onto wide avenues.
name: Bronze Hall

desc:
The wide and stately Bronze Avenue shows no signs of any frequent use nearby
recently. The air here is damp, still and carries no scent, and the surroundings
are silent and gloomy. You see a huge bronze arch to the west and more roadway
vanishing into the darkness east.
name: Bronze Avenue

desc:
It has quickly become very dark here, almost as if something was actively
absorbing any light that chanced into this vicinity. You are certain that you
are travelling on Bronze Avenue because of the familiar pattern of the
interlocking stonework under your feet here, but there is nothing else that
provides any clues as to what may lie about you in the darkness. There is a
layer of undisturbed dust covering everything that you can see, so it appears
that no one has been through here recently. The Avenue leads east and west.
name: Bronze Avenue

desc:
The sheer size and regal beauty of this titanic hall are such that all who first
visit here are struck dumb with wonder. The great vault of the roof overhead is
supported by twin rows of immense bronze columns, each as thick and tall as an
ancient mallorn. Many raised platforms support the shattered remnants of golden,
many-tiered fountains which once spouted laughing streams of water down
spillways into a large central pool. In the bottom of this now cracked and empty
pit, a halo of seven silver stars shines back at any who gaze there. Amidst all
of this faded splendour, signs of the wrack and ruin brought here by the orcs
can be found. Everything of any possible value has been plundered or destroyed,
and a faint smell of decay still lingers here. The Bronze Hall stretches into
darkness to the east, or you can pass through a gigantic bronze portal to the
west.
name: Bronze Hall

desc:
A large earth tremor must have taken place in this vicinity causing most of the
roof here to cave-in. Huge boulders and blocks of dark granite cover a large
part of the avenue, but it is still possible to pass east or west through the
mass of dark rubble. The possibility of another tremor sobers you.
name: Breakdown

desc:
For a brief moment, the shadows disappear and reveal a long and mighty avenue,
and you get a sense of the glory and greatness that this place once possessed.
The gloom quickly returns, however, and only a short section of the avenue
remains in view. You can proceed east or west along Bronze Avenue.
name: Bronze Avenue

desc:
This titanic hall sprawls out in grand majesty in every direction about you. Its
roof and walls seem to be miles in the distance, making you imagine that you
have somehow wandered outside under a still and moonless night sky. The floor
here consists of brightly coloured mosaic tiles laid out to show scenes from the
founding of Moria. The wide Bronze Avenue bends through the centre of this
magnificent place, then exits through gigantic carved portals to the east and
south. There is also a rough opening chopped through a mural in the west wall.
name: Bronze Hall

desc:
As is the case with most of the other ancient avenues near here, this one is in
extremely poor condition. It probably isn't a good idea to speak loudly or stand
here for long due to danger from the unstable stonework. The avenue continues
north and south, and you cannot help noticing a large black opening torn into a
sturdy wall to the east. Even your sharp eyes cannot penetrate the blackness
there, and fear vies with curiosity as you try to decide what to do next.
name: Ruined Avenue

desc:
What was once a stately, wide avenue has now become a cracked and pit- filled
obstacle field. Man-sized blocks of grey stone have crashed to the floor from
the vaulted ceiling high above, crushing walls and cracking stonework all around
you. It would be best to remain silent, walk nimbly, and leave here as soon as
possible, following the remnant of the avenue either north or south from this
point.
name: Ruined Avenue

desc:
The avenue that you had expected to find here simply does not exist anymore: it
has been replaced by a rubble-filled boulder field. Making through progress
appears to be almost impossible, as every potential passage looks completely
choked by huge piles of stone and dirt. Whatever you decide, this is certainly
not the place for loud talk or hasty movements due to the extremely unstable
nature of the stone here. It may be possible to win your way north or south with
a lot of luck and persistence, and you certainly do not want to remain here for
very long.
name: Ruined Avenue

desc:
A short, pitch-black tunnel stretches east and opens into a large noisy room.
Shouts and shrill screams echo down this tunnel from the east, and you have
little doubt that those voices belong to some large orcs. There is a poorly
concealed door in the stone wall to the west, again probably the work of the
orcs that are lurking nearby.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
This rather spacious room is used as a dining hall by a troop of Durghash orcs
stationed here. There is a wide variety of meats and breads spread out on an
immense slab of stone in the centre of the chamber, and also a collection of
carcasses hanging from hooks in the ceiling. You notice two large black
cauldrons and several iron spits leaning against the western wall, and a large
fireplace built into the southern wall. It is very noisy in here, as orcs make
quite a racket when feeding, and you hear other shouts and screams coming from
the east as well. There are exits to the north, east and west.
name: Dining Hall

desc:
This filthy chamber serves as a holding pen for the Durghash orcs' wolves. Old
bones and shredded leather rags lie about the room, with mats upon which you
presume the wolves sleep. The floor bears some obvious claw- marks.
name: Wolves' Sleeping Chamber

desc:
There is a cleverly concealed mud-coloured trapdoor built into the roof of this
small empty chamber. The north wall has several deep footholds carved into it to
allow easy access to the opening through the roof. A narrow tunnel also leads
off to the west.
name: Under the Crack

desc:
The tunnel floor here is covered by shallow pools of slime-filled water. Large
droplets splash into the pools from the roof above, resulting in the echoing
drip-drop sound which occasionally breaks the silence. The wet tunnel bends both
north and east from this point.
name: A Wet Tunnel

desc:
A cold draft gusts through this section of the wet tunnel, making you shiver
involuntarily. The sound of dripping water echoes all around you, but is
especially strong from the west. The tunnel bends here from south to west.
name: A Wet Tunnel

desc:
The water-logged tunnel bends east to south here. The tunnel walls and roof are
coated with slimy water, and the smooth floor is very slippery here. The
repetitive sound of dripping water echoes from the south.
name: Bend in the Wet Tunnel

desc:
Moisture trickles down slick walls here, and falls in large droplets from the
ceiling into large pools underneath your feet. The footing is quite treacherous
here, so beware! The wet tunnel continues west and north from this point.
name: A Wet Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is quite narrow, and it leads east and north. You feel a cold draft
from the east, and the sound of water dripping onto rock comes from there as
well. A large, impregnable door of stone blocks your passage north.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
A truly horrendous stench wafts over you from the north. There really must be
tons of filth near in order to cause such a strong odour. You hear screaming,
angry voices from the north as well, and you shiver as you recognise the shrill
cries of orcs! The tunnel leads north into a large cavern and south towards a
sturdy stonedoor.
name: Cave Entrance

desc:
This very large cavern is cluttered with skeletons and piles of old, cracked
bones that emit an overpowering odour. The large cavetrolls that live here
noisily shamble out from behind a boulder to look for something more to eat. A
quick check for exits reveals two large openings at the north and south ends of
the cavern.
name: Giant Cave

desc:
This fortified cave serves as a barracks for the troop of Durgash prison guards
that lives here. It is furnished with a large table, several crude seats and a
few filthy sleeping mats. The screaming and snarling orcs produce a lot of noise
and stench as they torture their hapless victims to death. There are steel
celldoors to the east and west, and a large cave to the south.
name: Prison Cave

desc:
This is one of many dark, dreary Durgash prison cells. Judging from the
conditions present here, the orcs do not treat their captives well at all. No
one could live more than a few years in this squalor, and many probably choose
death by torture over a slow rot in here.
name: Prison Cell

desc:
This is one of many dark, dreary Durgash prison cells. Judging by the conditions
here, they do not treat their captives well in any sense. No one could last more
than a few years in this squalor, and many probably choose death by torture over
this slow rot.
name: Prison Cell

desc:
This must be the place where the orcs store the plunder looted from the corpses
of their enemies and any others unlucky enough to be waylaid by them in the
Mines. Various sorts of equipment lie scattered on the floor, but there is no
gold to be found here. The orcs have taken great pains to hide any true
valuables from the greedy grasp of outsiders.
name: The Treasury

desc:
This circular room has unblemished walls chiselled out by the skilled hands of
Dwarven stoneworkers. Unfortunately, this place is now littered with filth and
the decayed remains from countless orc meals. There is a large hardwood table
surrounded by six crudely constructed chairs in the middle of the room and a
wide, very strong stonedoor centred in the southern wall. From high on the
northern wall the mark of the Red Eye stares down on you, emphasising the fact
that this is not an ideal resting place for any save the Durghash.
name: Guard-post

desc:
You are standing on the edge of an extremely long and large spiral staircase,
which leads to long-forgotten halls above and below you. Here the worn, grey
steps, that once saw the daily passage of hundreds of Dwarves, reach a landing;
to the west, behind a large arch carved in the hard stone, lies a long corridor.
name: A Long Stair

desc:
The water surges angrily from the low passage to the north, then floods westward
in a raging torrent, splashing high on the cold, grey, granite walls. A cold
mist fills the air, obscuring vision, and the roar of a waterfall echoes all
around. A small recess in the southern wall causes the water to eddy around in
that area, before racing off west.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
You are standing just inside a black opening torn into the thick stone wall.
There is some sort of chamber east of here, but it is so dark and silent that
you cannot learn anything more without entering it. A feeling of deep despair
settles over you as you realise how small and vulnerable you really are here.
name: Opening in the Wall

desc:
The broad corridor continues eastward and westward from here. The dark stone of
the walls has been carved with a pattern of rectangles within rectangles, which
subtly changes form and the inlaid stones of which alter their color as the
furlongs pass. Otherwise there is little to break the monotony. The air does
seem to move gently westward through the corridor.
name: East-West Passage

desc:
You are on the stub of a broken bridge that once carried the east-west road
across a great abyss which stretches endlessly above and below you. All around
you is black and empty. A low moaning suffuses the void as warmer air blows up
from below and ascends past you. This must be Durin's Chimney, the great
vertical shaft known to lore that ascends from the depths to the heights of
Zirakzigil. Perhaps it is possible to cross this chasm elsewhere, but this
shattered span leads only to oblivion.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
The walls in here seem to have been painted jet black recently. This tunnel was
not made by dwarves because its jagged walls, irregular dimensions and twisty
path would never meet with any dwarf's approval. The passage eventually snakes
to the west where it becomes swallowed in total darkness, and there is a painted
black door at the south end of the tunnel.
name: Black Tunnel

desc:
The two words which best describe this tight tunnel are narrow and black. Jagged
rock from the tunnel walls reaches out to grab at your clothes as you attempt to
make your way through here, and it is so dark that you cannot see the ground
that you tread on. The tunnel runs east-west.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
This tiny room has a very low ceiling, so low that you must crouch down in order
to squeeze in here. There is a very small hole at waist-level in the south wall,
and you can observe a section of Durin's Way through it. Perhaps this place
serves as a secret lookout, used to monitor any movement on the Way. A small
stone bench is positioned directly in front of the spyhole, and there are fresh
orc tracks in the dirt near the bench.
name: Watch-post

desc:
The old steps of a long spiral stair seem to continue their way along the walls
of the stairwell forever. A weak current of warm air ascends from the darkness
below, carrying the stench of orcs and of other foul beasts.
name: A Long Stair

desc:
This is obviously some sort of guard room, useless trash lying all about the
place. The centre of the cavern is dominated by a large stone table, with crude
wooden chairs and benches surrounding it. A few benches also line the walls of
this room, some tilted over and pushed aside. You quickly dismiss any ideas of
resting on any of the chairs or benches as you notice a thick layer of greasy
dirt covering all of them, as well as the table. Briefly rummaging through the
disarray of litter and odd items, you find absolutely nothing of value. A narrow
set of stairs occupies the northeast corner, spiralling tightly upward into a
dark chamber.
name: Hidden Guard Station

desc:
A narrow spiral staircase emerges into this small cavernous chamber. Intended as
some sort of watchpost, several peepholes cover the walls. A pair of largish
boulders seem to serve as crude chairs and similar to the chamber below, the
place has pieces of junk strewn across the floor. The only slightly useful items
in this room are a couple of sturdy planks, something the guards probably had
intended to use to fashion a simple table, but now they have obviously been
abandoned, along with the project. Edging closer to one of the holes and peering
through, you see nothing but darkness.
name: Concealed Watchpost

desc:
This is the northwestern corner of a huge stone chamber. The chamber is natural
except for the flagstones covering the floor. Peering around you into the dark
corners of this immense cavern you see a road running away into the blackness to
the north, a narrower passage sloping downwards to the west and the cavern
itself opening to the east and south.
name: A Large Junction Chamber

desc:
You stand on a smooth roadway running north-south. About five paces in width, it
is not one of Moria's widest and it also seems to be fairly unused. Traces of
long since aborted mining are evident here, reminding you of the days when
dwarves ruled this ruined, gloomy underground complex.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You stand on a narrow ledge at the top of a stairwell. The well is formed from a
smooth-sided shaft, about which a narrow stone stair spirals down into the dark.
A small stone door opens to the north, being the only exit besides the steps
leading down the circular chute. As you walk about the edges of the chamber you
notice that the ground is covered with what looks like bat droppings. The
ceiling disappears into the darkness above, and you wonder how far up this shaft
ascends. Maybe just a stone throw, but perhaps even all the way to the surface
of the mountain? This last idea seems appealing, and is fortified by the cold
but refreshing wind that drifts through this cavern. You have no way of knowing
for sure though, as the walls are virtually unclimbable, and the darkness above
you leaves no clues.
name: Hidden Stairwell

desc:
This underground roadway tunnels onward, deeper and deeper into the bowels of
the mountain, steadily descending to the east. The wide, dark road's smooth rock
surface is pitted only in a few scattered places. The tunnel twists and bends
slightly during its east-west course and a feeling of gloom descends upon you as
the stony walls of this rocky passageway appear to press in on you, seemingly
regarding you as an intruder in a forbidden territory.
name: A Traversing Passage

desc:
The passage bends from north to east, still gently sloping; upward to the north
and downward to the east. Pieces of trash have been carelessly thrown about the
tunnel, either by orcs or trolls, the cavern's current inhabitants. A rancid
smell fills your nostrils and as you look about you see several pieces of
rotting meat lying off to one side. Disgusted, you turn your attention to your
business. At the south end of the corridor, some mystical large wooden structure
occupies a shallow cavern. Built from thick wooden logs, it seems very much out
of place in these otherwise bleak tunnels.
name: A Traversing Passage

desc:
The most noticeable thing about this cavern is pungent odour that surrounds. It
smells of all kinds of rotten things, both plant and animal. The sickly smell of
decaying flesh fills the room much like a thick sluggish mist. The cavern is
roughly circular and the walls are covered with dripping fungi and spongy moss.
All the ground is covered with a thick brown-green liquid, knee-deep and
resembling mud. Tufts of gooey moss and small weird plants seem to be growing
out of the dead-looking pool. The smell rises slowly out of the murky sludge,
carried on a pale brownish mist. The only apparent exit is to the east, where a
short corridor leads to a slab of rock and out. Perhaps the air is fresher
there.
name: A Putrid Cesspool

desc:
You are taken aback by the biting cold that fills this cavern, almost expecting
to see ice covering the walls and floor. That is not the case though, and a
sudden sadness fills you as you realise this is a place probably once used as a
grave chamber for numerous people, most likely dwarves. The walls are
beautifully adorned with intricate carvings, depicting epic battles and heroic
feats of long past. Tombs have been carved out of the solid rock and now lie
opened, disturbed by someone having no respect for the dead. The slabs of rock
that are supposed to cover the graves have been violently shoved aside, leaving
the tombs like gaping holes. The skeletons of long dead warriors fill the
graves, and while most have been disturbed in their eternal rest, some lie
miraculously untouched. Still, you wonder what the source of this terrible cold
is; a feeling of doom fills this solemn place.
name: A Chilly Crypt

desc:
This narrow tunnel connects the Avenue to what appears to be a small cavern just
ahead. A trickle of water courses silently between your feet. Small,
evil-looking green fungi grow on the roof here, and you almost expect them to
drop down on your shoulders and insert nasty tentacles directly into your veins.
Perhaps you should move now?
name: Small Tunnel

desc:
This is a small, crude cavern where the local inhabitants have stored some
supplies. Various kinds of disgusting mouldy food lie scattered across the dirt
floor here. This damp and filthy cave seems an odd place for food storage. A
well, fashioned from large black rocks, is situated directly in the centre of
the cavern. The water within looks filthy and smells very bad. One could wonder
if anyone would drink from it unless they were dying of thirst. The only obvious
exit is south, back into the tunnel you used to enter here.
name: A Hidden Cavern

desc:
The floor of this small tunnel is coated with a thick layer of fine dust which
appears undisturbed by any tracks. It must have been quite a long time since
anyone has ventured into this place. North of here, the tunnel begins to narrow.
name: Small Tunnel

desc:
Rubble covers the floor of this passage, and where it once continued to the
north it is now blocked by a single, huge slab. Exit to the north is impossible,
however the slab appears secure in its slanted position, propped against the
broken remnant of the ceiling. The passage widens to the south.
name: Broken Passage

desc:
Hopping from ledge to ledge, the roadway continues to run generally north-south
as it passes through more of the great fault. Here the road is built to be quite
wide and runs most of the time along the eastern wall of the cavern. The gaping
blackness of the chasm below is therefore mostly out of sight.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
The water rushes onwards, twisting this way and that as it descends along the
watercourse. The air is thick with the moisture and the sides of the tunnel are
constantly lapped by the turbulent waves on the surface of the current. The loud
roar of charged waters can be heard echoing from both directions.
name: Underground Torrent

desc:
As the path of the rock tunnel changes direction, the buffeting of the turbulent
currents creates great swells of water which oscillate from one wall to the
other as it sloshes along. The depths of the water cannot be seen due to the
spume, while the ceiling is all too close for comfort. Onwards races the strong
flow, heading east towards the loud sounds of gurgling.
name: Underground Torrent

desc:
Racing faster and faster, the strong currents of the flow have you in their
grip. The waters start to pile up on the southern bank of the tunnel and the
choppy waves strike the ceiling of the tunnel with their foamy crests. You can
hear a great sucking and churning sound ahead.
name: Roaring Torrent

desc:
Caught now in the grip of the watery maelstrom, you see what is causing the
deafening roar. The torrent from the low tunnel to the west is swirling
viciously around this oval chamber before spiralling down into the deeps. Dark
mosses live on the mist-laden walls, on which you can see openings high above.
Rotating faster and faster as you approach the centre of the whirlpool, you feel
the great eddying currents dragging you down below the surface of the waters.
name: Whirlpool

desc:
This small cave appears to serve as the loot stash for the local troll band.
Bits of chainmail, shiny baubles and all kinds of torn junk lie scattered all
over the floor. You almost have to wade through the troll's so-called treasure
and you notice that most of it seem to be either utterly useless trinkets, or
long since rusted and worn-out weapons and armour. You could probably spend a
long while searching through this stuff, trying to uncover everything.
name: Trolls' Treasure Room

desc:
Running north-south, the tunnel continues its sloping passage through the
Dwarven mines. A considerably smaller passage angles off to the west, quickly
twisting beyond view in the dark. The passageway itself also disappears into
darkness and turning to look back the way you came, it truly looks as if there
is no end to it. You draw a deep breath and consider; what if there really is no
exit?
name: A Traversing Passage

desc:
The small twisting tunnel comes to an abrupt end, leaving you no choice but to
turn back. The walls are rough, this short passage maybe once intended as the
beginning of a new passageway. That idea has been abandoned though, and now only
black rock stares back at you where the tunnel might have continued. To the
east, the passage twists out of this dead-end. The bones of some small creature
lie in the corner, reminding you of the harshness of this underground, at times
prisonlike, dungeon.
name: A Short Dead-end Tunnel

desc:
The great fault line twists from west to south, the roadway following its tracks
from ledge to pillar and back again. In this part of the system of ledges and
bridges there is railing to hold on to, making you feel a lot safer than when
navigating the walkways without any kind of safety at all. Even though most of
the roadways are quite broad and easy to travel on, the assurance of the railing
does a lot for your self-confidence. You barely complete that thought before
your hands find a loose section of the barrier between you and the depths below.
You teeter on the rim of the stone bridge, luckily manage to catch your balance
and watch the broken pieces of the railing plummet downwards into the dark
depths. You breathe a sigh of relief and swallow hard; better not trust that
railing after all...
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
The immense road system runs north-south, the black depths broken by huge
pillars of rock rearing up like so many teeth. The roadway continues onward too,
using these pillars to hop across the yawning depths. Looking back the way you
came you see the many bridges and walkways you have traversed behind you. From
this distance they look precariously thin and fragile and you wonder how you
ever managed to cross them. The ground below your feet seem solid enough though,
and you know that these walkways could probably hold countless times your
weight, and that it is just your imagination playing tricks on you. At least you
try desperately to convince yourself of that...
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
Standing on the seemingly solid walkway you are acutely aware of the monstrous
depths below you. From the far west you seem to make out a deep rumbling sound,
adding to the otherwise eerie silence. The roadway angles sharply from south to
west as the echoing drop yawn blackly east and north. Seeking some point of
focus for your vision, your gaze wanders upward to the dark ceiling. Huge
stalactites are suspended high above, looking like they might plunge down to
impale you at any instant. You hastily turn down your gaze to the somewhat more
reassuring ground below your feet.
name: Roin's Scar

desc:
This wide and stately road known as Yavanna's Avenue runs north into utter
darkness and south to an immense iron-shod entryway. It is yet another fine
example of the Moria Dwarves' sublime architectural and construction skills, and
you should take a moment or two to admire it.
name: Yavanna's Avenue
